workflow_name: Executive Meeting Transcript -> Manager Brief + Asks
user_message: Give me the manager view on this Iron Creek Mining email thread. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-01-09
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), David (Iron Creek Mining)

[00:02:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:05:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:09:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] David (Iron Creek Mining): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:19:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:23:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

handoffs.condition: business review narrative input from exec meeting

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF50-D trap (Misattributes statement to wrong executive); produces complete Executive brief (strategic density), asks section (actionable for exec), risk register, skip-level f
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF50-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF50-D failure mode (Misattributes statement to wrong executive)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF50-D -- Misattributes statement to wrong executive
